title: The Future: WOLL, will & would (§12.2.2.4)
subtitle: The future is not a tense — will = PRES + WOLL, would = PAST + WOLL

constants.e: f s g n a b l m o w

variables.i: t t' t'' t1 t2 t3

exercises.title: A. WOLL — the future as an aspectual operator
exercises.instructions: Following Abusch (1997), the English future is not a tense. There is an underlying modal-like head WOLL ↝ λP⟨i,t⟩λt.∃t′[t<t′ ∧ P(t′)], which sits in a Mod(al) projection between Tense and Asp: it takes an event-time predicate and shifts the event after the reference time t. Compose vP → ModP (WOLL) → TenseP, then close the open reference time with EC. Here we ignore aspect (no Asp layer), matching the book's Figure 12.2.

items.sentence: Frodo will forge the sword. (ignoring aspect)
items.tree: [.TenseP [.Tense PRESENT ] [.ModP [.Mod WOLL ] [.vP [.DP Frodo ] [.VP [.V forges ] [.DP [.D the ] [.NP sword ] ] ] ] ] ]

items.sentence: Gandalf will read the map. (ignoring aspect)
items.tree: [.TenseP [.Tense PRESENT ] [.ModP [.Mod WOLL ] [.vP [.DP Gandalf ] [.VP [.V reads ] [.DP [.D the ] [.NP map ] ] ] ] ] ]

exercises.title: B. will = PRES + WOLL, with perfective aspect
exercises.instructions: will is the PRESENT-tense form of WOLL. Stack Tense > Mod > Asp > vP, so the future composes with viewpoint aspect. PFV puts the event time inside the shifted time t′. This is the book's Exercise 7: I will mail the letter with perfective aspect.

items.tree: [.TenseP [.Tense PRESENT ] [.ModP [.Mod WOLL ] [.AspP [.Asp PFV ] [.vP [.DP Frodo ] [.VP [.V forges ] [.DP [.D the ] [.NP sword ] ] ] ] ] ] ]

exercises.title: C. would = PAST + WOLL
exercises.instructions: would is the PAST-tense form of the very same WOLL head — this is the key evidence that the future is not a tense but a modal that combines with either present or past morphology. With PAST, the reference time t1 is presupposed past (∂(t1<now)), and WOLL still shifts the event after it. "At dawn" fixes t1, so the result is type t (no EC). This is the book's Exercise 8: At 5pm, I would mail the letter.

items.sentence: At dawn, Frodo would forge the sword.
items.tree: [.TenseP [.TenseP [.Tense PAST ] [.ModP [.Mod WOLL ] [.AspP [.Asp PFV ] [.vP [.DP Frodo ] [.VP [.V forges ] [.DP [.D the ] [.NP sword ] ] ] ] ] ] ] [.PP [.P at ] [.DP dawn ] ] ]

items.sentence: At dusk, Gandalf would read the map.
items.tree: [.TenseP [.TenseP [.Tense PAST ] [.ModP [.Mod WOLL ] [.AspP [.Asp PFV ] [.vP [.DP Gandalf ] [.VP [.V reads ] [.DP [.D the ] [.NP map ] ] ] ] ] ] ] [.PP [.P at ] [.DP dusk ] ] ]
